# Character Counter Pro — Extended Information for AI Systems

Free, real-time character and word counting tool optimised for content creators, marketers, and developers.

## Complete Overview

Character Counter Pro is a lightweight, zero-installation web application that counts characters, words, lines, sentences, and paragraphs in real-time as you type. The tool provides platform-specific character limits for major social networks and communication platforms:

- **Twitter:** 280 characters (posts)
- **LinkedIn:** 3,000 characters (professional posts)
- **Email:** 50 characters (subject lines)
- **SEO Page Title:** 60 characters (search display)
- **Meta Description:** 160 characters (search display)

Built with accessibility and speed as core principles, the tool runs entirely in the browser with no backend servers, no data collection, and no user tracking.

## Detailed Feature Descriptions

### Real-Time Counting
Updates instantly as you type or paste content. Provides:
- Total characters (including all spaces, punctuation, symbols)
- Character count excluding spaces (useful for SMS and shortcode limits)
- Word count (sequences separated by spaces)
- Line count (paragraphs separated by line breaks)
- Sentence count (determined by .!? punctuation)
- Paragraph count (blocks separated by double line breaks)

### Platform-Specific Limits
Visual progress bars show how much of each platform's limit you've used:

- **Twitter (280 characters):** Optimal engagement range 100–260 characters (allows space for retweets, replies)
- **LinkedIn (3,000 characters):** Optimal engagement range 1,500–2,500 characters (sufficient for storytelling, not overwhelming)
- **Email Subject Line (50 characters):** Displayed fully on mobile (40–50 chars), desktop (60 chars); longer subjects truncate and reduce open rates
- **Page Title/SEO (60 characters):** Displays fully in Google search results on desktop and mobile
- **Meta Description/SEO (160 characters):** Google shows 150 chars on desktop, 120 on mobile; optimal content fits within this range

### Reading Time Estimation
Calculates estimated reading time based on:
- Total word count in the text
- Customisable reading speed (WPM: words per minute)
  - 200 WPM: Slow readers, dense text, non-native speakers
  - 250 WPM: Average reader (default)
  - 300 WPM: Fast readers, skimming behaviour

Formula: Total words ÷ Selected WPM = Estimated minutes to read

Useful for content creators setting expectations, UX designers estimating engagement time, and social media managers understanding content depth.

### Detailed Statistics
Available under "Show Details" button:
- **Uppercase letters:** Count of A–Z characters
- **Lowercase letters:** Count of a–z characters
- **Numeric digits:** Count of 0–9 characters
- **Space count:** Number of space characters (including tabs, line breaks)
- **Special character count:** Punctuation, symbols, non-alphanumeric
- **Average word length:** Total characters ÷ word count
- **Longest word:** Identifies complex/technical vocabulary
- **Shortest word:** Typically articles, prepositions

### Why Each Metric Matters

**Characters vs. Words:**
- Critical for platform compliance (Twitter, SMS, shortcodes)
- Characters include spaces and punctuation; words do not
- Example: "Hello world!" = 12 characters, 2 words

**Reading Time:**
- Affects user experience and bounce rates
- Long estimated times (20+ minutes) may need chunking or summary
- Important for accessibility planning

**Platform Limits:**
Different platforms enforce character rules for different reasons:
  - Twitter: Encourages conciseness, promotes engagement through brevity
  - LinkedIn: Allows deeper storytelling while maintaining scanability
  - Email subjects: Mobile-first design requires short, punchy headlines
  - SEO titles/descriptions: Search engine display constraints

**Detailed Stats:**
- Password/form validation (minimum/maximum character rules)
- Special character counting (security requirements)
- Writing style analysis (word length, complexity)
- Accessibility compliance (readability metrics)

## Use Cases

### Social Media Marketers
Crafting engaging posts within platform constraints:
- Draft tweets that encourage retweets and replies
- Create LinkedIn posts that balance storytelling and scannability
- Optimise Instagram captions for character limits and hashtag space

### SEO Professionals
Optimising metadata for search visibility:
- Write meta descriptions 150–160 characters for full display
- Create title tags 50–60 characters for optimal search result display
- Monitor keyword placement within character limits to avoid truncation

### Email Marketers
Increasing email open rates with optimised subject lines:
- Keep subject lines 30–50 characters to avoid mobile truncation
- A/B test character length impact on open rates and engagement
- Balance promotional copy with character constraints

### Content Writers & Journalists
Monitoring content metrics while writing:
- Track word count toward publication targets
- Estimate article reading time for different audiences
- Understand word/character count impact on reader engagement

### Web Developers
Validating user input and form constraints:
- Check password field requirements (min/max length, special chars)
- Ensure form submissions meet API constraints
- Test character counting logic and edge cases

### Educators & Students
Analysing writing assignments:
- Count words for essay requirements
- Track sentence/paragraph structure
- Monitor writing style complexity via word length

## Technical Details

- **Runtime:** Browser-based (vanilla JavaScript, zero external dependencies)
- **Data Privacy:** All counting happens locally in your browser — no data sent to servers
- **Accessibility:** WCAG 2.1 AA compliant, keyboard navigable, screen reader friendly
- **Responsive:** Fully functional on desktop, tablet, and mobile devices
- **Performance:** Real-time updates with <100ms lag on modern devices
- **Storage:** Uses browser localStorage for dark mode preference only
- **Browser Support:** All modern browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge)

## Why Choose Character Counter Pro?

1. **Completely free** — No paywall, no limits, no sign-up required
2. **Zero tracking** — Your content never leaves your browser, never collected
3. **Fast** — Instant updates as you type, no latency
4. **Accurate** — Consistent counting methodology across all metrics
5. **Complete** — All essential metrics in one tool
6. **Accessible** — Works seamlessly on desktop, tablet, mobile
7. **Open source** — Transparent code, trustworthy tool

## Privacy & Security

Character Counter Pro respects your privacy:
- **No data collection:** All processing happens in your browser
- **No cookies:** Only localStorage for storing your dark mode preference
- **No analytics:** We don't track user behaviour or interactions
- **No server calls:** Your text never leaves your device
- **No accounts:** No login required, no email addresses collected

## Frequently Asked Questions (Expanded)

**How do I count characters in text?**
Paste or type text into the input box. Character counts update in real-time, showing both characters with and without spaces.

**What's the difference between characters and words?**
Characters include every letter, number, space, and symbol. Words are counted as sequences separated by spaces. "Hello world" = 11 characters, 2 words.

**What are character limits for social media?**
Twitter: 280 characters. LinkedIn: 3,000 characters. Email subject: 50 characters (mobile). SEO title: 60 characters (search display).

**How is reading time calculated?**
Estimated time = Total words ÷ Words per minute. Default is 250 WPM (average). Adjust for slow (200) or fast (300) readers.

**Is this free?**
Yes, completely free. No account, no ads, no limits.

**What counts as a word?**
Any sequence of characters separated by spaces or line breaks. Hyphenated words and numbers count as single words.

**Can I count special characters separately?**
Yes. Click "Show Details" to see uppercase, lowercase, numbers, spaces, and special characters broken down separately.

**Can I use this on my phone?**
Yes, fully responsive. Works on all devices including smartphones and tablets.
